chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 5830 of 2024: Shakeel Ahmed Jokhio vs. Federation of Pakistan & Others For the Petitioner/s: Messrs. Saathi M. Ishaque and S.K. Lodhi, Advocates For the Respondent/s: Ms. Alizeh Bashir, Assistant Attorney General of Pakistan Date/s of hearing: 19.12.2024 Date of announcement: 19.12.2024 ORDER Agha Faisal, J. The petitioner, represented to be an ex-contingent employee of the respondent no. 2, has filed this petition seeking to assert possessory rights in respect of Government accommodation. No subsisting contract of employment has been demonstrated before us and even otherwise a contractual right, if any, is not amendable for agitation in writ jurisdiction. Reliance is placed upon an allotment letter purportedly issued back in 2013 and notwithstanding the tenancy thereof, not discernible from the record annexed, clause 4 thereof clearly demonstrates the absence of any proprietary and / or possessory right in respect of the relevant accommodation. Be that as it may, the memorandum of petition demonstrates that the petitioner is even unwilling to pay the rates / charges with respect to the property, however, seeks the sanction of this court to approve his possession irrespective thereof. In view hereof, this petition is found to be misconceived, hence, hereby dismissed, along with pending applications, in limine. Judge Judge
